alerts.title: Iran launches attack on Kuwait; ceasefire frays across multiple fronts
alerts.summary: Iran reportedly launched a direct attack on Kuwait airport as Israel and Lebanon renew ceasefire amid simultaneous Israeli strikes killing 12 including officers, per CBS News, Al Jazeera, and AP News. Multiple concurrent exchanges in Persian Gulf and Israel-Lebanon border indicate cascade of ceasefire violations and escalating proxy pressure.

alerts.title: Iran 60% enrichment unmonitored; IAEA verification gap persists amid conflict
alerts.summary: UN nuclear inspectors remain unable to verify Iran's enriched uranium stockpile and enrichment activities since fighting escalated, per Reuters and multiple nuclear headlines. Iran maintains 60% U-235 enrichment with estimated 1-2 week weapons-grade breakout window. US drafting IAEA resolution demanding Iran account for bombed nuclear sites, but verification gap creates acute proliferation risk.

alerts.title: Houthis escalate Red Sea attacks; Iran coordination confirmed on tanker targeting
alerts.summary: Houthis conducting sustained maritime attacks in Red Sea with confirmed reporting that Iran pressures group to target oil tankers specifically, per South China Morning Post and Courthouse News. Four separate incidents reported with 3 crew killed; Houthi-Iranian coordination signals deliberate economic pressure on global shipping and proxy escalation signal.

alerts.title: US concentrates three destroyers in Red Sea as Houthi threat escalates
alerts.summary: USS Carney, USS Mason, and USS Laboon positioned in Red Sea alongside USS Eisenhower carrier and USS Bataan amphibious ship, per OSINT asset tracking. Destroyer concentration signals US readiness to defend shipping lanes against intensifying Houthi-Iranian maritime campaign.

alerts.title: Trump claims Iran retains only 21-22% of missile capacity after US strikes
alerts.summary: Former President Trump stated Iran has lost 78-79% of its missile and drone capacity in recent air campaign, per TODAY.com and Jerusalem Post. If accurate, claim indicates significant degradation of Iranian strike capability, though assessment awaiting independent ISW or US military confirmation. Statement may signal US confidence in weakening Iranian retaliation options.

analysis_note: The US-Iran conflict theater shows critical fragmentation: nuclear breakout window narrowing amid verification gaps, ceasefire mechanics failing across three regional fronts (Persian Gulf, Israel-Lebanon, Red Sea), and proxy coordination intensifying while Iran's conventional strike capacity reportedly degraded—creating asymmetric escalation pressure.
